title: Pentaphylloides parvifolia
intro: Pentaphylloides parvifolia, also known as Fringed Pentaphylloides or Alpine Rose, is a flowering plant species belonging to the family Rosaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, offering various health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, wounds, and infections. The bioactive constituents of Pentaphylloides parvifolia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Pentaphylloides parvifolia is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture to support overall health and well-being.
taxonomy: Pentaphylloides parvifolia is classified within the family Rosaceae. This family includes a wide range of flowering plants commonly known as rose or apple family. Pentaphylloides parvifolia is further classified within the subfamily Rosoideae, which contains many shrubs and small trees. The subfamily Rosoideae also includes other species such as Fragaria and Potentilla. Pentaphylloides parvifolia's specific classification is also related to its unique characteristics and phylogenetic relationships.
uses: Pentaphylloides parvifolia, also known as the small-leaved pentaphylloides or creeping pentaphylloides, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes.
